response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

***  

the approach to solve this problem is to iterate through each element of the array and calculate the required sum based on the given conditions. for each index i, if i is a multiple of 3, we calculate the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements. otherwise, we calculate the sum of absolute differences.  

to efficiently compute the sum of absolute differences, we can use the prefix sums of the sorted array. for a sorted array, the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all elements before it can be computed using the prefix sums. this allows us to calculate the sum of absolute differences in O(1) time per element.  

for the square of the difference, we can use a similar approach but compute the sum of squared differences using the prefix sums. this approach ensures that the solution is efficient and handles the constraints effectively.  

k appears at least 2 times in the response.
